Name: PV Soiling Shading Snow

Caption.String: Self Shading for Fixed Subarrays and One-axis Trackers

Snow losses are caused by snow covering the array. When snow depth data is available in the weather file or provided below, SAM can estimate these losses for each subarray. Click Edit Array to enter your own data, or click Download Snow Data to download data from the NOHRSC.

External shading losses account for the reduction in beam and diffuse irradiance incident on the array caused by nearby objects such as trees and buildings. Use the Edit Shading buttons below to edit and import shading data for each subarray. Use the 3D Shade Calculator to draw a representation of the array and nearby objects and automatically calculated shading data. Data may be entered by hand, imported from shading analysis software and devices, or generated by the 3D Shade Calculator.

Self shading accounts for shading of modules in the subarray by modules in neighborning rows. Linear self-shading represents self shading as a reduction in beam irradiance incident on the subarray. Non-linear self shading represents self shading as a reduction in the diffuse irradiance incident on the subarray and the subarray DC output.
Enable and disable subarrays on the System Sizing page.
POA irradiance losses account for soiling and other factors that reduce the total solar irradiance incident on the array.

Items.StringList: Enter or download time series snow data|Use snow data from weather file

use_snow_weather_file.IndexLabels: Enter or download time series snow data|Use snow data from weather file

Callbacks: msg = 'Weather file has no snow data. Simulation will fail.';
// check whether the input array is the same length as the weather file
msg = 'Weather file time step (' + to_string(wfp) + ' minutes) is different from snow data time step (' + to_string(ts) + ' minutes).';
msg = 'Snow depth data contains negative values. Click Edit Array to see the data.';
msg = 'Snow depth data contains no values greater than zero. Click Edit Array to see the data.';
msg = 'Click Edit Array to display snow data.';
// hide inputs for disabled subarrays (always show subarray 1 inputs)
// hide until we implement a bifacial rack shading calculation
property( 'nohrsc_url', 'caption', 'View data on the NOHRSC website' );
//msgbox('Debug\nShade data written to CSV (t/f): ' + cwd()+'/shade-data.csv' + '\n' + csvwrite(cwd()+'/shade-data.csv', x));
if ( x.ierr == 0 ) // shade calculator has valid data
// check that number of subarrays in shade scene matches enabled subarrays
msg = 'WARNING: You have ' + n_subarrays + ' subarrays enabled and ' + x.nsubarrays + ' subarrays in the 3D scene.\n';
msg += 'Update timeseries shading and diffuse losses for ' + n_subarrays + ' subarrays with calculated values from 3D scene?\n\n';
// check that time series shading data is available for this subarray
// if subarray is not enabled apply shading data from next subarray in shading data
// note that subarray number in time series data is not the same as active surface subarray property but order is preserved
// check parallel strings = number of subarray strings
if (!yesno('WARNING!\nFor ' + pre + ', you specified ' + value(pre + '_nstrings') + ' strings and have ' + #timestep[0] + ' strings for this subarray in the 3D scene.\nApply shading data anyway?'))
msgbox('Shading data applied!\nClick Edit Shading for each enabled subarray to check the data.');
